text: The patient is a sixty-one year old lady with an history of repeated kidney transplantation, parathyroidectomy for adenoma, who underwent total abdominal hysterectomy and bilateral salpingophorectomy with lymphadenectomy in two thousand sixteen. In twenty eighteen she was diagnosed with the rectovaginal fistula. The decision of a multidisciplinary committee was to perform a diverting colostomy to delay repair after patient optimization. After an initial improvement, symptoms worsened. An MRI scan in July twenty nineteen confirmed the rectovaginal fistula.

The patient is positioned in a prone jackknife position with legs open. The robotic system is positioned on the right side, with ports placed along the jet point path, including an air seal port. The surgeon uses robotic bipolar forceps to gently lift the rectal wall, preparing the rectovaginal septum around the visible blue orifice using diathermy. The dissection is carried out cephalad, developing the plane laterally and carefully dissecting the distal portion.

The orifice in the vaginal wall is highlighted in red. Single stitches are used to close the vaginal wall, completing the suture. A biologic mesh is placed over the vaginal suture. The rectum is repaired using a V-lock stitch for the running suture.
and this is the final result. The patient was discharged on postoperative day three after undergoing planned hemodialysis.
